pi_items.source: Found in both setup_task.sh and export_result.sh defining the required output location.
Downloaded standard ImageJ sample t1-head.zip from https://imagej.net/images/t1-head.zip and verified width using tifffile.
Downloaded standard ImageJ sample t1-head.zip from https://imagej.net/images/t1-head.zip and verified height using tifffile.
Downloaded standard ImageJ sample t1-head.zip from https://imagej.net/images/t1-head.zip and verified frame count using tifffile.
Checked task description ('Scale the image spatial dimensions to 75%') and verifier.py logic.
Checked task description ('keep only every 3rd slice') and verifier.py logic.
Verified against scoring variables in verifier.py: `tol_dim = metadata.get('tolerance_dim', 5)`.
Verified against scoring variables in verifier.py: `tol_depth = metadata.get('tolerance_depth', 3)`.

privileged_info_summary: I verified that the task uses the authentic 'T1 Head' MRI sample from ImageJ's sample dataset repository (t1-head.zip). Downloading and inspecting the TIFF dataset confirms its original dimensions are 256x256 pixels with a depth of 129 slices, and a 16-bit uint16 data type. The task requirements correctly match the verifier logic to convert to 8-bit, scale to 75% size (roughly 192x192), subsample to every 3rd slice (roughly 43 slices), and output the result to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/processed/mri_preview.tif.
